text: Universal AI is a theoretically well-understood endpoint that can ground analysis of the AGI-to-ASI transition.
ASI can be treated as systems more intelligent and cognitively capable than large human organizations.
Four plausible pathways from AGI to ASI are scaling AGI, paradigm shifts, recursive improvement, and large-scale multi-agent collectives.
Friction and bottlenecks along these pathways create concrete open research questions about whether progress will remain rapid or slow materially.
A single transformative step-change framing may be less accurate than a series of transformative AI-enabled changes across science and technology.
